mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects aggregate income expansion that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in the top PAYEMS growth quartile compared to the bottom quartile with a Mann-Whitney U test p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy raises the cost of capital enough to trigger negative equity repricing over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS above 5% are not negative according to a one-sided t-test with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month deceleration in retail sales and acceleration in consumer prices creates a stagflationary impulse where revenue growth slows while discount rates rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL changes in a predictive regression fails to achieve statistical significance with a p-value above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, accelerating industrial production signals a cyclical rebound in capacity utilization that leads to positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, positive INDPRO shocks are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in real retail sales forewarns declining consumer demand that compresses corporate top-line growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly declines fail to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a directional hit rate greater than 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production alleviates supply constraints and reduces input cost pressures, supporting margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following 3-month INDPRO increases above 1.0%, average 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive according to a t-test with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside a declining federal funds rate signals a recessionary policy response where earnings deterioration outweighs discount rate relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2 percentage points over 2 months and FEDFUNDS decreases over same period
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered episodes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts reflects weakening residential investment and construction employment, preceding broader demand contraction that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following month-over-month HOUST drops exceeding 5%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year backtest.
